notes: v1.14.42 - globe atmosphere/arc restraint. A dropped-in "realism" guide was otherwise three.js + a sun/day reversal of the v1.14.33 night-earth (re-add sunlight, mask city lights to the dark side) - skipped all of that (kept the night look + the v1.14.40/41 city-light cloud John approved) and took only the compatible restraint tweaks, translated to the raw WebGL. (1) Toned down the blue rim: GFS fresnel atmosphere-rim intensity 0.55 -> 0.38 + the canvas atmosphere-edge gradient stop 0.32 -> 0.20 (softer, less neon outline). (2) Eased the data arcs so they sit under the city-light cloud, not over it: arc point size 1.6 -> 1.25 + color 0.30,0.52,0.65 -> 0.21,0.36,0.46 (dimmer cyan). UNCHANGED: city-light cloud (fine + realism color, v1.14.40/41), terrain, hub beacons, orientation, sun-killed night base, wordmark, status, ENTER_ENABLED (still false), service worker. JS/GLSL-only (numeric constants; the fresnel shader recompiles - render-verified) -> CSS <style> braces unchanged 3468/3468. Gates: node --check 3/3 inline + sw.js PASS, four stamps lockstep v1.14.42, cache bumped, dct/sw CRLF + version.json LF. DEVICE-CHECK (John, iPhone, full close+reopen ONLINE to clear SW cache): blue limb rim softer/thinner (less neon), arcs more restrained under the lights, rest as v1.14.41. Easy to push the rim/arcs further if still too strong - or revisit a real sunlit-earth pivot separately if that is the actual goal.
